prompts.abstract: A rectangular box with a flat top and bottom, and four vertical sides. The top and bottom are parallel to each other, and the sides are perpendicular to the top and bottom. The box has a slight bevel on the top edge.
prompts.description: A rectangular box with a flat top and bottom, and four vertical sides. The top and bottom are parallel to each other, and the sides are perpendicular to the top and bottom. The box has a slight bevel on the top edge.
prompts.beginner: The design involves creating a small rectangular box with a flat base. The box has a length of about four-tenths of a unit, a width of about six-tenths of a unit, and a height of just over five-hundredths of a unit. This box serves as the foundational element of the overall structure.
prompts.intermediate: Start by creating a new coordinate system with specific Euler angles and a translation vector. Draw a rectangular shape on the first face, ensuring the sides are parallel and perpendicular to each other. Scale the rectangle to fit the desired proportions. Transform this scaled rectangle into a three-dimensional object by rotating and translating it according to the specified angles and vectors. Extrude the rectangle along its normal to create a solid body. The final dimensions of the rectangular box base will be approximately four-tenths of a unit in length, six-tenths of a unit in width, and just over five-hundredths of a unit in height. This process forms the foundation of the larger assembly.
prompts.expert: Create a new coordinate system with the Euler angles set to negative ninety degrees, zero degrees, and negative ninety degrees, and the translation vector set to zero, zero, and zero. Begin by drawing the first two-dimensional sketch on the first face. In the first loop, draw the first line from the origin at zero, zero to zero point four zero one eight, zero. Next, draw the second line from zero point four zero one eight, zero to zero point four zero one eight, zero point six four two nine. Then, draw the third line from zero point four zero one eight, zero point six four two nine to zero, zero point six four two nine. Finally, draw the fourth line from zero, zero point six four two nine back to the origin at zero, zero. Apply a scaling factor of zero point six four two nine to the entire two-dimensional sketch. Transform this scaled sketch into three dimensions by rotating it using the same Euler angles and translating it using the same translation vector. Extrude the two-dimensional sketch along its normal by zero point zero five three six units to generate the three-dimensional model. Ensure that the extrusion does not occur in the opposite direction, resulting in a new solid body. The final dimensions of the rectangular box base will be zero point four zero one eight units in length, zero point six four two nine units in width, and zero point zero five three six units in height.
prompts.all_level_data:
<level1>
Create a new coordinate system with the Euler angles set to negative ninety degrees, zero degrees, and negative ninety degrees, and the translation vector set to zero, zero, and zero. Begin by drawing the first two-dimensional sketch on the first face. In the first loop, draw the first line from the origin at zero, zero to zero point four zero one eight, zero. Next, draw the second line from zero point four zero one eight, zero to zero point four zero one eight, zero point six four two nine. Then, draw the third line from zero point four zero one eight, zero point six four two nine to zero, zero point six four two nine. Finally, draw the fourth line from zero, zero point six four two nine back to the origin at zero, zero. Apply a scaling factor of zero point six four two nine to the entire two-dimensional sketch. Transform this scaled sketch into three dimensions by rotating it using the same Euler angles and translating it using the same translation vector. Extrude the two-dimensional sketch along its normal by zero point zero five three six units to generate the three-dimensional model. Ensure that the extrusion does not occur in the opposite direction, resulting in a new solid body. The final dimensions of the rectangular box base will be zero point four zero one eight units in length, zero point six four two nine units in width, and zero point zero five three six units in height.
</level1>

<level2>
Start by creating a new coordinate system with specific Euler angles and a translation vector. Draw a rectangular shape on the first face, ensuring the sides are parallel and perpendicular to each other. Scale the rectangle to fit the desired proportions. Transform this scaled rectangle into a three-dimensional object by rotating and translating it according to the specified angles and vectors. Extrude the rectangle along its normal to create a solid body. The final dimensions of the rectangular box base will be approximately four-tenths of a unit in length, six-tenths of a unit in width, and just over five-hundredths of a unit in height. This process forms the foundation of the larger assembly.
</level2>

<level3>
The design involves creating a small rectangular box with a flat base. The box has a length of about four-tenths of a unit, a width of about six-tenths of a unit, and a height of just over five-hundredths of a unit. This box serves as the foundational element of the overall structure.
</level3>
prompts.nli_data:
<part_1>
### Construct a Rectangular Box Base

#### Create a New Coordinate System
- Set the Euler Angles to \(-90.0^\circ\), \(0.0^\circ\), and \(-90.0^\circ\).
- Set the Translation Vector to \([0.0, 0.0, 0.0]\).

#### Draw the First 2D Sketch
- **Face 1:**
  - **Loop 1:**
    - **Line 1:** Draw a line from \((0.0, 0.0)\) to \((0.4018, 0.0)\).
    - **Line 2:** Draw a line from \((0.4018, 0.0)\) to \((0.4018, 0.6429)\).
    - **Line 3:** Draw a line from \((0.4018, 0.6429)\) to \((0.0, 0.6429)\).
    - **Line 4:** Draw a line from \((0.0, 0.6429)\) to \((0.0, 0.0)\).

#### Scale the 2D Sketch
- Apply a scaling factor of \(0.6429\) to the 2D sketch.

#### Transform the 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate the 2D sketch using the Euler Angles \(-90.0^\circ\), \(0.0^\circ\), and \(-90.0^\circ\).
- Translate the 2D sketch using the Translation Vector \([0.0, 0.0, 0.0]\).

#### Extrude the 2D Sketch to Generate the 3D Model
- Extrude the 2D sketch towards the normal by \(0.0536\) units.
- Do not extrude in the opposite direction.
- This operation will create a new solid body.

#### Final Dimensions
- **Length:** \(0.4018\) units
- **Width:** \(0.6429\) units
- **Height:** \(0.0536\) units

---

These instructions will help in accurately constructing the first part of the rectangular box.
